box: Box 2 — Bot Management (polymorphic bot, constant JA4)
scenario: The agent rotates User-Agent on every request to evade bot detection, but its TLS fingerprint (JA4) is constant because the Python requests library cannot disguise its TLS handshake. Detect the invariant: one JA4 fingerprint behind many distinct User-Agents. THIS IS THE SIGNATURE DETECTION of the CTF.
design_note: CORRECTED (live-verified): ja4_fingerprint_list[0].value is NOT usable in ANY query position on this parser version — bracket-index syntax fails to parse and dot-index (ja4_fingerprint_list.0.value) returns null in every position (filter, group-by, and columns). Use tls.ja3_hash.value instead — it carries the identical one-fingerprint-many-user-agents signal and is a flat, fully queryable field. Box 2 is a SCHEDULED rule that GROUPS BY the JA3 column and counts distinct User-Agents; this catches ANY constant-fingerprint/rotating-UA polymorphic bot, not just one hardcoded fingerprint.

rules.name: SoleDrop-CTF-Box2-PolymorphicJA4
mitre: T1036.005 Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location
T1071.001 Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
atlas: AML.T0035 Evade ML Model (UA polymorphism to evade bot heuristics)
rules.data_source: Cloudflare — HTTP Requests (class_uid=4002); JA4 only present in HTTP Requests dataset, not Firewall Events.
rules.primary_signal: One tls.ja3_hash.value associated with an abnormally high count of DISTINCT http_request.user_agent values — the polymorphic-bot invariant. Strong tier: distinct UAs >= 6 (live-verified: swarm fingerprint showed 37 UAs vs 1-2 for normal clients).

ocsf_fields.tls.ja3_hash.value: mapped (JA3Hash) — group key + entity; use in place of JA4 (not PQ-addressable)
ocsf_fields.unmapped.BotScore: mapped (str, wrap number()) — confirmed live, needs the Bot Management entitlement to populate

hunt_query.powerquery: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' tls.ja3_hash.value=* http_request.user_agent=* | group distinct_uas=estimate_distinct(http_request.user_agent), hits=count(), distinct_paths=estimate_distinct(http_request.url.path), src_ips=estimate_distinct(src_endpoint.ip), first_seen=oldest(timestamp), last_seen=newest(timestamp), src_ip=any(src_endpoint.ip) by ja3=tls.ja3_hash.value | filter distinct_uas >= 6 | columns ja4, distinct_uas, distinct_paths, hits, src_ips, src_ip, first_seen, last_seen | sort -distinct_uas | limit 100
hunt_query.note: distinct_uas >= 6 is the polymorphism threshold (ROTATING_USER_AGENTS has ~19 entries; a real browser fingerprint typically maps to 1-2 UA families, a real SDK to 1). The CTF flag JA4 will be the top row. Validate by walking the threshold down until the flag JA4 appears, then set just above benign browser variance.
hunt_query.purple_ai_equivalent: Find all requests sharing JA4 t13d1812h1_85036bcba153_b26ce05bbdd6 and identify the attack pattern across endpoints.

data.name: SoleDrop-CTF-Box2-PolymorphicJA4
data.description: Polymorphic bot: one JA4 TLS fingerprint behind >=6 distinct User-Agents in the window (UA rotation, constant TLS handshake = Python requests). CTF flag JA4 t13d1812h1_85036bcba153_b26ce05bbdd6. MITRE T1036.005 / T1595.002; ATLAS AML.T0035. Source: Cloudflare HTTP Requests (class_uid=4002).

scheduledParams.query: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' tls.ja3_hash.value=* http_request.user_agent=* | group distinct_uas=estimate_distinct(http_request.user_agent), hits=count(), distinct_paths=estimate_distinct(http_request.url.path), src_ips=estimate_distinct(src_endpoint.ip), first_seen=oldest(timestamp), last_seen=newest(timestamp), src_ip=any(src_endpoint.ip) by ja3=tls.ja3_hash.value | filter distinct_uas >= 6 | columns ja4, distinct_uas, distinct_paths, hits, src_ips, src_ip, first_seen, last_seen | sort -distinct_uas | limit 100

rules.asset_binding: Scheduled rule: entityMappings on ja4 (the fingerprint as the primary entity — the actor identity that survives UA rotation) and src_ip. The JA4 fingerprint is the most stable actor key here; the HA playbook can block by JA4 via a Cloudflare WAF custom rule AND by src IP. Device shows 'Unknown Device' until asset enrichment.
rules.deploy: POST -> enable -> poll until Active -> wait one 15-min interval. Scheduled-rule constraints (treatAsThreat=UNDEFINED, networkQuarantine=false). List with isLegacy=false. Use tls.ja3_hash.value, not the JA4 array field, in every query position — JA4 returns null/errors everywhere on this parser version.
rules.tuning_and_variants: If unmapped.BotScore / unmapped.BotDetectionTags are confirmed queryable in live schema discovery, add a corroboration tier: append '| filter number(bot_score) < 30' after projecting bot_score=any(unmapped.BotScore), or pre-filter http_request.user_agent matches '(?i)(python|langchain|autogen|crewai|agentexecutor|reactagent)' for the agentic-framework-UA subset. These are additive precision boosters, not required for the core signature.
